items.title: New Israeli airstrikes put Gaza truce in question | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Israeli airstrikes have killed at least 24 people in Gaza, including several children, according to Gaza health officials. It's been six weeks since the ceasefire between Israel and Hamas took effect. But well over 300 people have been killed there since. Both sides have accused each other of violating the ceasefire deal.

items.title: Can European allies still adjust US' Ukraine plan in upcoming Geneva talks? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has appointed a delegation to look at US President Donald Trump's proposals to end the war with Russia. Western leaders have said they accept the plan as a basis for further negotiations -- but that more work is needed. Trump has pressured Ukraine to accept the agreement by next Thursday. But the US president appeared to hint there was still room to negotiate when speaking to reporters on Saturday. 
_
00:00 US plans details Ukrainian concessions for ceasefire 
02:38 Domitilla Sagramoso, Senior Lecturer in Security and Development at King's College London
06:45 Kira Rudik, Leader of Ukraine's pro-European Holos party and a member of parliament in Kyiv

items.title: Wealthier nations should triple funding against effects of climate crisis, COP30 agrees | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Delegates at the United Nations climate talks in Brazil have reached a compromise deal - though it doesn't detail how the world will move away from fossil fuels. 

The hosts acknowledged that some countries had greater ambitions for the COP30 summit and said a fossil fuel transition plan would come in a separate proposal, published later. Delegates did, however, reach agreement over funding.  Wealthier nations should at least triple the amount given to developing countries to cope with the effects of climate change.   

00:00 Climate talks end without roadmap on fossil fuel phase-out
00:46 Tim Schauenberg, DW Climate Reporter in Belem 
03:26 Niklas Höhne, New Climate Institute Co-founder 

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Ukraine allies push back on US 'peace plan' largely favoring Russia | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>News of a US proposed 'peace plan' that largely favours Russia is overshadowing the G20 leaders' summit currently underway in South Africa. The first G20 gathering in Africa was meant to discuss helping poor nations. But the US proposal on Ukraine blindsided the European Union leaders in attendance with Trump, Xi and Putin absent from the summit. 
EU leaders had held emergency talks on the sidelines of the summit. 

More work is needed on the US-Russian negotiated proposal to end the war in Ukraine, western leaders say. This comes after US President Donald Trump announced he is giving Ukraine until next Thursday to accept the 28 point proposal. The White House has reportedly threatened to withdraw US support for Ukraine if the deadline is not met. 
_
00:00 US plan for Ukraine casts shadow on G20 
01:02 Friedrich Merz, German Chancellor on ending Ukraine war 
01:46 Michaela Küfner, DW Chief Political Editor in Johannesburg
05:16 William Courtney, Former US Ambassador on US plan 

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Schools in Nigeria on lockdown after abduction of 300 students | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Schools in parts of Nigeria are on lockdown after more than 300 students and teachers were abducted from a Catholic boarding school, according to a local Christian organization. Gunmen stormed the school in the north-central state of Niger state early on Friday in the second mass school abduction in Nigeria in a week. No group has claimed responsibility for the abductions, which are often carried out for ransom payments. DW's Azeezat Olaoluwa reports from Lagos.

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.author: DW News
items.content:

items.title: How Morocco's Western Sahara gamble paid off | Mapped Out
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Morocco occupies most of disputed Western Sahara while the indigenous Sahrawi people's movement, the Polisario Front, backed by Algeria, seeks an independent state. But Morocco has managed to win increasing global support - by using its phosphate resources to foster long-term partnerships in Africa and striking deals with the US under Donald Trump. Morocco also has good relations with Europe, including the former colonial powers Spain and France, promoting further support for its territorial claims.

items.title: NewsClash over fossil fuels threatens COP30 resolution in Brazil | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>A battle over fossil fuels is threatening to sink the UN climate summit in Brazil. Negotiators at COP30 have deadlocked over a proposal to phase out fossil fuels. The talks went into overtime as delegates worked through the night to find a compromise that would allow the summit to issue a final communique. Host Brazil had pushed for a so-called roadmap to end the use of fossil fuels but it was removed from the text under pressure from a group of more than 80 countries including oil producers Saudi Arabia and Russia. The phaseout was a key demand by some of the countries most affected by climate change.

00:00 Nations at odds over fossile fuel phaseout at COP30
00:41 DW speaks with Rana Adib who heads up REN21, a renewable energy policy network supported by the United Nations Environment Programme

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/
